heading: Failure to provide reports on certain tax-favored accounts or annuities; penalties relating to designated nondeductible contributions
label: 6693 Failure to provide reports on certain tax-favored accounts or annuities; penalties relating to designated nondeductible contributions
notes: Amendments2025—Subsec. (a)(2)(G). added subpar. (G).Pub. L. 119–212014—Subsec. (a)(2)(E), (F). added subpar. (E) and redesignated former subpar. (E) as (F).Pub. L. 113–2952003—Subsec. (a)(2)(C) to (E). added subpar. (C) and redesignated former subpars. (C) and (D) as (D) and (E), respectively.Pub. L. 108–1732001—Subsec. (a)(2)(C). substituted “qualified tuition” for “qualified State tuition”.Pub. L. 107–16Subsec. (a)(2)(D). substituted “Coverdell education savings” for “education individual retirement”.Pub. L. 107–222000—Subsec. (a)(2)(B). substituted “Archer MSAs” for “medical savings accounts”.Pub. L. 106–5541998—Subsec. (a)(2)(C), (D). substituted “section” for “Section”.Pub. L. 105–2771997—, substituted “certain tax-favored” for “individual retirement” in section catchline.Pub. L. 105–34, § 211(e)(2)(C)Subsec. (a). , inserted concluding provisions.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1602(a)(4)Subsec. (a)(2)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 105–34, § 211(e)(2)(B)Subsec. (a)(2)(D). , added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 105–34, § 213(c)Subsec. (c)(2). , inserted “and issuer” before “penalties” in heading and “or issuer” before “who fails” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1601(d)(1)(C)(ii)1996—Subsec. (a). inserted heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The person required by subsection (i) or () of section 408 to file a report regarding an individual retirement account or individual retirement annuity at the time and in the manner required by such subsection shall pay a penalty of $50 for each failure unless it is shown that such failure is due to reasonable cause. This subsection shall not apply to any report which is an information return described in section 6724(d)(1)(C)(i) or a payee statement described in section 6724(d)(2)(W).”Pub. L. 104–191l, inserted at end “This subsection shall not apply to any report which is an information return described in section 6724(d)(1)(C)(i) or a payee statement described in section 6724(d)(2)(W).”Pub. L. 104–188, § 1455(d)(3)Subsecs. (c), (d). , added subsec. (c) and redesignated former subsec. (c) as (d).Pub. L. 104–188, § 1421(b)(4)(B)1988—, substituted “penalties relating to” for “overstatement of” in section catchline.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1011(b)(4)(B)(i)Subsec. (b). , substituted “Penalties relating to” for “Overstatement of designated” in heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Any individual who—Pub. L. 100–647, § 1011(b)(4)(A)“(1) is required to furnish information under section 408()(4) as to the amount of designated nondeductible contributions made for any taxable year, ando“(2) overstates the amount of such contributions made for such taxable year,shall pay a penalty of $100 for each such overstatement unless it is shown that such overstatement is due to reasonable cause.”1986—, inserted “; overstatement of designated nondeductible contributions” in section catchline.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1102(d)(2)(B)Subsec. (b). , added subsec. (b). Former subsec. (b) redesignated (c).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1102(d)(1)Subsec. (c). , (2)(A), redesignated former subsec. (b) as (c) and substituted “this section” for “subsection (a)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1102(d)(1)1984—Subsec. (a). substituted “$50” for “$10”.Pub. L. 98–3691980—Subsec. (a). substituted “subsection (i) or () of section 408 to file” for “section 408(i) to file”, and “such subsection shall pay” for “section 408(i) shall pay”.Pub. L. 96–222l

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If a person required to file a report under a provision referred to in paragraph (2) fails to file such report at the time and in the manner required by such provision, such person shall pay a penalty of $50 for each failure unless it is shown that such failure is due to reasonable cause.

children.label: A
children.text: subsections (i) and () of section 408 (relating to individual retirement plans),l

children.label: F
children.text: section 530(h) (relating to Coverdell education savings accounts), and

children.heading: Penalties relating to nondeductible contributions
children.label: b Penalties relating to nondeductible contributions
A
children.text: is required to furnish information under section 408()(4) as to the amount of designated nondeductible contributions made for any taxable year, ando

children.label: B
children.text: overstates the amount of such contributions made for such taxable year,

children.heading: Failure to file form
children.label: 2 Failure to file form
children.text: Any individual who fails to file a form required to be filed by the Secretary under section 408()(4) shall pay a penalty of $50 for each such failure unless it is shown that such failure is due to reasonable cause.o

children.heading: Penalties relating to simple retirement accounts
children.label: c Penalties relating to simple retirement accounts

children.heading: Employer penalties
children.label: 1 Employer penalties
children.text: An employer who fails to provide 1 or more notices required by section 408()(2)(C) shall pay a penalty of $50 for each day on which such failures continue.l

children.label: A
children.text: to provide 1 or more statements required by the last sentence of section 408(i) shall pay a penalty of $50 for each day on which such failures continue, or

children.label: B
children.text: to provide 1 or more summary descriptions required by section 408()(2)(B) shall pay a penalty of $50 for each day on which such failures continue.l

children.heading: Reasonable cause exception
children.label: 3 Reasonable cause exception
children.text: No penalty shall be imposed under this subsection with respect to any failure which the taxpayer shows was due to reasonable cause.

children.heading: Deficiency procedures not to apply
children.label: d Deficiency procedures not to apply
children.text: Subchapter B of chapter 63 (relating to deficiency procedures for income, estate, gift, and certain excise taxes) does not apply to the assessment or collection of any penalty imposed by this section.
